text:
1. **In `rpc_who` (`src/daemon/server/who.rs:66-94`)**, where the caller's session already resolves for `(self_slug, self_pubkey)`: also compute `let instance = state.session_instance(&rec)` and attach a small `self` identity object to the `out` JSON (`label = instance.display_slug()`, `pubkey = instance.pubkey`, `is_member`, `pending`, `host`, `rel_cwd`) — reusing the exact field logic from `rpc_whoami` (who.rs:130-161). Use `self_pubkey = instance.pubkey` rather than `rec.agent_pubkey` so the `(you)` member match (summary.rs:148) keys on the ordinal-selected pubkey (issue #98 correctness).
2. **In `render_fabric_view` / `render_snapshot_body` (`src/cli/who/awareness.rs:43-105`)**, prepend a one-line self header ("You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host})") before `Project:`/`Channel:`, driven by the new `self` block. This makes `tenex-edge who`, when run inside an agent session, answer "who am I, which channel, my instance label/pubkey" — exactly `whoami`'s job. [...] Assistant: Decision: **remove `whoami` entirely**, fold self-identity into `who`. [...] Issue #99 slice complete. `whoami` is fully removed and its self-identity folded into `who`; the codename disambiguation is gone from the renderers.
 [...] - Attaches an `out["self"]` object with `label`, `pubkey`, `channel` (=`rec.channel_h`), `host`, `is_member` (`s.is_channel_member(&rec.channel_h, &instance.pubkey)`), `working`, `status` (=`rec.title`), `pending` (read-only `drain_pending_for_session`), `created_at`, and a RAW `session_id` for correlation. **No `codename` field.**
 [...] - Deleted `display_row_agent_name`, `agent_name_counts_for_scope`, and `agent_count_key` (the entire codename-disambiguation apparatus).
- `render_who_row` and `render_who_markdown_row` now render `row.slug` directly (concurrent same-agent instances carry distinct ordinal slugs, e.g. `haiku`/`haiku1`); their `name_counts`/`split_counts_by_project` params and the corresponding setup in `render_who_once`/`render_who_plain` were removed. Removed the `session_codename` reference. [...] - Deleted `render_whoami`; replaced it with `render_self_header(v) -> Option<String>` driven by `out["self"]` — a concise leading line `You are **{label}** on **{channel}** ({host}).` plus `pubkey … · status … · member yes/no · N pending`. Never shows a codename or raw session id.
- Wired the header into both `who` (printed before the fabric block) and `who_live` (prepended to the live fabric screen). [...] - Rewrote `agent_renderer_disambiguates_duplicate_slugs_as_agent_names` → `agent_renderer_renders_ordinal_slugs_directly` (slugs `codex`/`codex1` render as-is; asserts no `codex-` suffix and no raw session id).
- Rewrote the `render_whoami_card_*` test → `render_self_header_names_self_by_label_without_session_code` (asserts label/channel/host header, pubkey, status, member, pending; no raw `sess-abc`), plus `render_self_header_absent_without_self_block`. [...] - **whoami removed entirely**; self-identity folded into `who` (a `self` block + header).
